data.content:
## Implementation Plan v4 (addressing all review feedback)

### Problem
Knot aliases (`alias` field from `kno ls/show --json`) are not carried through the data pipeline or displayed in Foolery views. Aliases provide human-readable names for knots; not showing them makes the UI confusing.

### Review Gap Resolutions

**Gap #1 — RelationshipPicker removability in lightbox (RESOLVED)**

The reviewer flagged that showing existing (persisted) dependencies as selected badges with X buttons implies removability, but `onRemove` only clears local state — there is no `DELETE /api/beats/[id]/deps` endpoint.

**Resolution: Keep existing deps read-only in the lightbox.** Specifically:
- Do NOT change the RelationshipPicker interface for this feature.
- The lightbox already loads persisted deps into local state and renders them as badges. The alias display change in RelationshipPicker is purely cosmetic (show `alias ?? shortId` on badge text) and does not change add/remove behavior.
- The existing `onRemove` local-state-only behavior is a pre-existing UX issue, not introduced by this plan. We will NOT add dependency removal functionality as part of this alias-display feature.
- To avoid confusion: mark persisted dep badges as read-only (no X button) by introducing an optional `readOnly?: boolean` flag on individual selected items. Newly-staged items keep the X button. This makes the lightbox safe without needing a DELETE endpoint.

**Concrete change:** Extend `RelationshipPicker` props from `selectedIds: string[]` to `selectedItems: { id: string; alias?: string; readOnly?: boolean }[]`. The `SelectedBadges` sub-component renders `alias ?? stripPrefix(id)` and conditionally hides the X button when `readOnly` is true. Parent callers:
- `beat-form.tsx`: All selections are staged (user is creating a new beat), so `readOnly: false` for all items.
- `beat-detail-lightbox.tsx`: Deps loaded from API get `readOnly: true`; newly-added deps during the session get `readOnly: false`.

**Gap #2 — cascade-close-dialog alias display (RESOLVED)**

The reviewer flagged that `CascadeDescendant` only carries `{id, title, state}` and `getOpenDescendants()` doesn't have alias data.

**Resolution: Extend CascadeDescendant to carry alias.** The function `getOpenDescendants()` already calls `getBackend().list()` which returns full `Beat` objects. Once Beat has an `alias` field (Layer 1), we simply pass `alias` through when building CascadeDescendant objects. Minimal change:
- Add `alias?: string` to `CascadeDescendant` interface
- In `getOpenDescendants()`, map `beat.alias` into the returned descendant object
- In `cascade-close-dialog.tsx`, display `d.alias ?? shortId(d.id)` alongside `d.title`

### Complete Change Plan

#### Layer 1: Type Definitions

1. **KnotRecord** (`src/lib/knots.ts`): Add `alias?: string | null` to interface
2. **Beat** (`src/lib/types.ts`): Add `alias?: string` after `id` field
3. **WaveBeat** (`src/lib/types.ts`): Add `alias?: string` after `id` field
4. **BeatDependency** (`src/lib/types.ts`): Add `alias?: string` — alias of the linked beat
5. **CascadeDescendant** (`src/lib/cascade-close.ts`): Add `alias?: string`

#### Layer 2: Backend Mapping

6. **toBeat()** (`src/lib/backends/knots-backend.ts`): Map `knot.alias ?? undefined` → `beat.alias` in BOTH code paths (with-workflow and fallback)

7. **listDependencies()** (`src/lib/backends/knots-backend.ts`): Enrich BeatDependency with alias of linked knot via `showKnot()` for each unique linked ID. O(n) calls but n is typically < 10.

8. **getOpenDescendants()** (`src/lib/cascade-close.ts`): Map `beat.alias` into CascadeDescendant when building results from the Beat list.

#### Layer 3: API Routes

9. **/api/waves/route.ts**: FIX dependency filter from `dep.dependency_type !== "blocks"` to `dep.type !== "blocks"` (critical correctness bug — currently ALL deps are silently skipped). When building WaveBeat objects, pass `alias: b.alias`.

#### Layer 4: OpenAPI Schemas

10. **schemas.ts**: Add `alias: { type: "string" }` to Beat, WaveBeat, and BeatDependency schema properties.

#### Layer 5: View Components

Display strategy: show `alias ?? stripPrefix(id)` everywhere IDs are displayed.

11. **beat-columns.tsx**: ID column — show `row.original.alias ?? shortId`
12. **beat-detail-lightbox.tsx**: Dialog header — show alias alongside short ID. Pass persisted deps as `{ id, alias, readOnly: true }` and staged deps as `{ id, alias, readOnly: false }` to RelationshipPicker.
13. **dep-tree.tsx**: Use `dep.alias ?? stripPrefix(linkedId)` in badge (works because Layer 2 enriches BeatDependency).
14. **wave-planner.tsx**: BeatCard — use `beat.alias ?? shortId(beat.id)`. Blocker references use alias from aliasMap.
15. **retakes-view.tsx**: RetakeRow — show alias if present.
16. **relationship-picker.tsx**: Refactor to accept `selectedItems: { id: string; alias?: string; readOnly?: boolean }[]`. SelectedBadges shows `alias ?? stripPrefix(id)` and conditionally hides X button when `readOnly`. SearchDropdown results show alias when available.
17. **beat-form.tsx**: Update RelationshipPicker usage to pass `selectedItems` (all with `readOnly: false` since beat-form is for new/editing beats).
18. **cascade-close-dialog.tsx**: Show `d.alias ?? shortId(d.id)` alongside title in descendant list.

#### Layer 6: Tests

19. **toBeat() unit test**: Verify alias maps correctly for null and non-null, both code paths
20. **listDependencies() unit test**: Verify BeatDependency includes alias; graceful when showKnot fails
21. **Waves API test**: Regression test for dep filter fix; verify WaveBeat includes alias
22. **relationship-picker test**: Verify SelectedBadges renders alias, falls back to short ID, hides X when readOnly
23. **cascade-close test**: Verify CascadeDescendant carries alias through getOpenDescendants

### Files Changed (~15 files)
- `src/lib/knots.ts` — KnotRecord type
- `src/lib/types.ts` — Beat, WaveBeat, BeatDependency types
- `src/lib/cascade-close.ts` — CascadeDescendant type + getOpenDescendants
- `src/lib/backends/knots-backend.ts` — toBeat(), listDependencies()
- `src/lib/openapi/schemas.ts` — API schemas
- `src/app/api/waves/route.ts` — Wave API: fix dep filter + alias passthrough
- `src/components/beat-columns.tsx` — ID column display
- `src/components/beat-detail-lightbox.tsx` — Dialog header + relationship-picker caller
- `src/components/dep-tree.tsx` — Dependency badge display
- `src/components/wave-planner.tsx` — BeatCard display
- `src/components/retakes-view.tsx` — RetakeRow display
- `src/components/relationship-picker.tsx` — Interface change + alias display + readOnly badges
- `src/components/beat-form.tsx` — relationship-picker caller update
- `src/components/cascade-close-dialog.tsx` — descendant alias display
- Test files (new or extended)

### Complexity
Medium — ~15 files, mostly mechanical. Non-trivial parts:
1. listDependencies() enrichment with linked-knot aliases (O(n) showKnot calls)
2. relationship-picker interface change (selectedIds → selectedItems) rippling to callers
3. Beat-detail-lightbox distinction between readOnly persisted deps and staged deps
4. Waves dep filter fix (critical one-line correctness bug)

### Risks
- Performance: Extra showKnot calls in listDependencies(). Mitigated by small dep counts.
- Backward compat: All alias fields are optional, existing consumers unaffected.
- The readOnly flag on RelationshipPicker keeps the lightbox safe without needing a DELETE endpoint.
